ml_D_D.graph package

Submodules

ml_D_D.graph.links module

graph/links.py DearPyGui node-editor link and delink callbacks.

ml_D_D.graph.links.delink_callback(sender, app_data) → None[source]

Handle removal of an existing link in the DearPyGui node editor.

This callback is triggered when the user deletes a connection between nodes. It performs the following operations: - Retrieves the currently active tab and its graph state. - Pushes the current state to the undo stack for rollback support. - Deletes the visual link from the node editor (if it exists). - Removes the corresponding link entry from the tab’s internal state.

Parameters:

  • sender – The DearPyGui node editor widget that triggered the callback.

  • app_data – The unique tag/identifier of the link to be removed.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.graph.links.link_callback(sender, app_data) → None[source]

Handle creation of a new link between two node attributes in the DearPyGui node editor.

This callback is triggered when the user connects two node pins. It performs the following operations: - Retrieves the currently active tab and its graph state. - Pushes the current state to the undo stack for rollback support. - Generates a unique link tag and creates the visual link in the node editor. - Stores the link mapping (source → destination) in the tab’s state. - Normalizes attribute identifiers (handles both integer IDs and string aliases). - Attempts to auto-propagate dimension information and validate compatibility

between connected nodes using the autofill engine.

Parameters:

  • sender – The DearPyGui node editor widget that triggered the callback.

  • app_data – A tuple/list containing the source and destination attribute IDs.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.graph.nodes module

nodes.py Node spawn and delete operations, both raw (no undo) and public (with undo).

Raw variants are used internally by the undo/redo snapshot system. Public variants are called from the UI and always push an undo snapshot first.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.attr_in_tag(tid: int, nid: int, pin: str) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.nodes.attr_out_tag(tid: int, nid: int, pin: str) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.nodes.attr_param_tag(tid: int, nid: int, param: str) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.nodes.clear_canvas() → None[source]

Delete every node on the active tab’s canvas.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.delete_node(ntag: str) → None[source]

Delete a single node from the active tab with undo.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.delete_selected_nodes() → None[source]

Delete all currently selected nodes in the active tab.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.input_field_tag(tid: int, nid: int, param: str) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.nodes.node_tag(tid: int, nid: int) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.nodes.raw_delete_node(tid: int, ntag: str) → None[source]

Delete a node and all its connected links from tab tid without touching the undo stack.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.raw_spawn_node(tid: int, block_label: str, nid: int | None = None, pos: tuple | None = None, params: dict | None = None) → str | None[source]

Spawn a node directly into tab tid without touching the undo stack. Used by snapshot restore, persistence load, and the public spawn_node() wrapper.

Parameters:

  • tid – target tab id

  • block_label – block type name

  • nid – force a specific node id (used by undo/load)

  • pos – (x, y) canvas position; auto-grid if None

  • params – dict of param_name -> value to pre-fill fields

Returns the node_tag on success, None if block_label is unknown.

ml_D_D.graph.nodes.spawn_node(block_label: str) → None[source]

Spawn a node into the active tab, pushing an undo snapshot first.

ml_D_D.graph.pipeline module

graph/pipeline.py Pipeline stage definitions and completion detection.

ml_D_D.graph.pipeline.get_stage_statuses() → list[dict][source]
ml_D_D.graph.pipeline.pipeline_ready() → bool[source]
ml_D_D.graph.pipeline.refresh_pipeline_bar() → None[source]

ml_D_D.graph.tabs module

tabs.py Tab lifecycle management; creation, closure, renaming, and the active-tab sync that runs every frame in the render loop.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.assign_role(tid: int, role: str | None) → None[source]

Assign or clear the pipeline role for tab tid. Updates the tab header colour and refreshes the pipeline bar.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.close_tab(tid: int | None) → None[source]

Close tab tid, cleaning up all its nodes and DearPyGui items.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.current_tab() → dict | None[source]

Return the dict for the currently active tab, or None.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.editor_tag(tid: int) → str[source]
ml_D_D.graph.tabs.new_tab(name: str | None = None, role: str | None = None) → int[source]

Create a new tab, add it to the tab bar, and make it active. role: “data_prep” | “model” | “training” | None Returns the new tab id.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.on_tab_change(sender, app_data) → None[source]

DearPyGui callback - fires when user clicks a tab. app_data is the integer item ID of the newly selected tab.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.open_assign_role_dialog() → None[source]

Show a popup to assign a pipeline role to the active tab.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.rename_tab(tid: int, new_name: str) → None[source]

Rename a tab both in state and in the DearPyGui label.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.sync_active_tab() → None[source]

Poll the tab bar every frame to keep state.active_tab_id in sync with whichever tab the user has clicked on.

dpg.get_value() on a tab_bar returns the internal integer ID of the selected tab item - so we resolve each tab’s tag to its item ID via dpg.get_alias_id() before comparing.

ml_D_D.graph.tabs.tab_tag(tid: int) → str[source]

ml_D_D.graph.undo module

undo.py Per-tab undo / redo stack using snapshots of node and link state.

Snapshots capture: block label, canvas position, and all param values. This means undo/redo fully restores a node including everything typed into it.

ml_D_D.graph.undo.push_undo(tid: int) → None[source]

Save a snapshot of tab tid onto its undo stack and clear its redo stack. Call this BEFORE any destructive operation.

ml_D_D.graph.undo.redo() → None[source]

Redo the last undone operation on the active tab.

ml_D_D.graph.undo.refresh_undo_menu() → None[source]

Grey out / enable the Edit > Undo and Edit > Redo menu items.

ml_D_D.graph.undo.undo() → None[source]

Undo the last operation on the active tab.

Module contents
